title: peritoma sonorae
intro: Peritoma sonorae, also known as the Sonoran globemallow, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can provide health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and preventing infections. The Sonoran globemallow can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
